label: p10_selected_moe_t16_launch_bounds_probe

hardware.note: local RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100; W7900 not rerun

scope: Selected-MoE T16 decode GEMV family: Q4_K gate/up direct and fused-SiLU direct kernels plus Q5_K/Q6_K down direct kernels. No selected-MoE Q8_0 T16 kernel is present in the current path; Q8_0 T16 is dense/shared and was handled separately.

code_audit.current_decode_dispatch: Runtime direct decode dispatch uses q4_k_t16_selected_dual_silu_direct_gemv_kernel for Q4 gate/up+SiLU, qk_t16_selected_direct_gemv_kernel<5> for Q5 down, and qk_t16_selected_direct_gemv_kernel<6> for Q6 down.
code_audit.already_fused: Q4 gate/up+SiLU is already fused in the direct decode path, avoiding separate gate/up output buffers and SiLU kernel for the hot path.
code_audit.q5_q6_launch_bounds: Q5/Q6 direct kernels already use __launch_bounds__(128,4) with 128-thread launches.

change_tested: Temporary launch-bounds alignment probe: changed q4_k_t16_selected_dual_direct_gemv_kernel and q4_k_t16_selected_dual_silu_direct_gemv_kernel from __launch_bounds__(256,2) to __launch_bounds__(128,4) to match their 128-thread direct launch. Change was reverted before commit because E2E decode did not improve.

validation.correctness_scope: Selected T16 synthetic correctness/dispatch tests, routing/graph policy tests, and finite logits in E2E benchmark.

commands.baseline: HIPENGINE_GGUF_AOTRITON_PREFILL=v3 HIPENGINE_GGUF_ALLOW_UNSAFE_QWEN35MOE_FASTPATHS=1 HIPENGINE_GGUF_DECODE_REPACK=1 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt PYTHONPATH=. uv run python scripts/qwen35_gguf_bench.py --model /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf --quant gguf_q4_k_m --prompt-length 4096 --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 1 --warmup-runs 1 --measured-runs 3 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --use-gemv-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --json /tmp/hipengine-gguf-selected-t16-baseline-4k-128.json
commands.candidate: same command after temporary launch-bounds change, --json /tmp/hipengine-gguf-selected-t16-launchbounds128-4k-128.json

decision: Rejected/reverted as not a speed win. Current obvious per-kernel launch-bounds cleanup is neutral/slightly negative at 4K/128 (-0.10% median decode, within noise but no upside). Next selected-MoE speed work should be structural: grouped/tiled MoE to reduce the ~120 selected-expert dispatches/token or combine Q4/Q5/Q6 expert work, not scalar metadata tweaks in the existing direct GEMV shape.
